labels.name: compare_pricing_pages
labels.description: Compare two to five public pricing pages side by side before you make competitive pricing or packaging claims. Use this when you want a quick, live comparison of visible prices, free-plan signals, and plan-name hints across vendors. The output is heuristic and page-level: it does not map every price

labels.name: compare_competitors
labels.description: Compare two or more exact package names side by side using live npm or PyPI metadata. Use this when you already know the candidate packages and need evidence for claims such as 'tool A is newer', 'tool B is still maintained', or 'these packages use different licenses'. It returns per-package registr

labels.name: verify_claim
labels.description: Check whether a factual claim is supported by a specific set of public evidence URLs that you already have. For each source, the tool performs a case-insensitive keyword match over the fetched page body, then marks that source as supporting the claim when at least half of the supplied keywords appea

labels.name: assess_compliance_posture
labels.description: Scan a public security, trust, compliance, or legal page for common enterprise buying signals before you claim a vendor supports a particular compliance posture. It looks for public references to SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA, DPA terms, subprocessors, SSO, SCIM, encryption, and data residency langu

labels.name: test_hypothesis
labels.description: Run a small verification plan made of concrete live checks and summarize whether a hypothesis is supported. Use this when one conclusion depends on multiple simple checks such as endpoint reachability, npm search counts, or whether a page contains an exact substring. This is a coordination tool, not

labels.name: create_monitor
labels.description: Create a persistent monitor that tracks a URL, pricing page, package version, endpoint status, vendor claim, or custom keyword pattern over time. Monitors run automatically on their configured schedule (hourly/daily/weekly) via the Cloudflare cron trigger, or on demand with run_monitor_now. Results

labels.name: list_monitors
labels.description: List all monitors owned by this API key, with last run status and schedule. Requires a team API key.

labels.name: run_monitor_now
labels.description: Immediately run a monitor's verification check outside its normal schedule. Records the result and returns whether the observed value changed since the last run. Counts against your monthly quota. Requires a team API key.

labels.name: get_monitor_result
labels.description: Retrieve the most recent run results for a monitor, including change details, confidence score, evidence URLs, and any error information. Requires a team API key.

labels.name: delete_monitor
labels.description: Permanently delete a monitor and all its stored results. This action cannot be undone. Requires a team API key.

labels.name: generate_change_report
labels.description: Generate a summary report of monitor activity for a time window. Shows monitors run, changes detected, failures, risk levels, and recommended follow-up actions. Requires a team API key.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: check_endpoint, check_pricing, inspect_security_headers, verify_claim, test_hypothesis, get_monitor_result · Private data: check_endpoint, list_resources, create_monitor, list_monitors, run_monitor_now, get_monitor_result, delete_monitor, generate_change_report · Public sink: check_endpoint, estimate_market · Destructive: check_pricing, test_hypothesis, create_monitor, list_monitors, run_monitor_now, get_monitor_result, delete_monitor, generate_change_report.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: check_endpoint, check_pricing, inspect_security_headers, verify_claim, test_hypothesis, get_monitor_result · Private data: check_endpoint, list_resources, create_monitor, list_monitors, run_monitor_now, get_monitor_result, delete_monitor, generate_change_report · Public sink: check_endpoint, estimate_market · Destructive: check_pricing, test_hypothesis, create_monitor, list_monitors, run_monitor_now, get_monitor_result, delete_monitor, generate_change_report

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
